topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a bright, unwelcome intrusion on my otherwise quiet evening. My stomach clenched instantly. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscle jump beneath my skin. I stared at the screen, the text—a casual comment about a recent class discussion—burning behind my eyes. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in my small room. I paced back and forth, my hands flexing, the frustration building into a hot, tight knot in my chest. Why? What was he thinking? I felt a surge of pure annoyance, a desperate need to lash out, but I forced myself to breathe, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of my desk. This was unacceptable.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked away in my phone's gallery—a photo I hadn't realized existed, a picture of me from a school event, captioned with a tag pointing directly to Mr. Harrison’s profile. A slow, deep burn started low in my gut and spread outward. I stood up abruptly, knocking my chair backward with a clatter that made me jump. My voice, when I finally spoke, was dangerously low, a strained whisper laced with disbelief. "Are you serious?" I didn't wait for a response. I snatched my phone back, my breathing shallow and quick, every nerve ending screaming with a sharp, agitated energy. I wanted to stomp out the screen, to erase the entire interaction, but my fingers were trembling too much to move decisively.

[story 3]

The realization hit me like a physical blow while I was waiting for the bus. It wasn't just a casual follow; it was a deliberate presence. My hands started shaking uncontrollably, a tremor running up my arms. I gripped the strap of my backpack until my fingers ached, my entire body rigid with suppressed agitation. I watched the phone screen, the familiar profile picture of my teacher staring back, and a fierce heat flooded my face. I could feel the blood pounding in my ears, a frantic rhythm mirroring the churning anxiety in my core. I took a deep, ragged breath, trying to steady the violent surge of feeling threatening to spill over into an outburst. I needed a plan, something sharp to cut through this infuriating situation.
